phrasal_verbs.meaning: stop people from entering an area by putting something such as a rope around it
move towards someone quietly and slowly, especially because you want to surprise them
move more slowly than other people so that you are behind them; make less progress or be less successful than other people who are doing a similar job or activity
become lost or go to the wrong place
prevent someone from going somewhere by getting in front of them; prevent something from taking place
stop someone or something from moving forwards
start living in a different house or flat (with)
leave one place and travel to another; stop discussing or doing something and begin discussing or doing something different
permanently leave the house or flat where you live or the place where you have your business
change your position in order to make space for someone or something
stop by the side of the road in a car or other vehicle
move so that sb can pass you; leave a job so that sb else can take over
visit somewhere before continuing to another place
(of an object) have one end move upwards, especially because something heavy has been put at the other end; turn a container upside down so that the things inside it come out
leave a meeting, performance, etc before the end, usually because you are angry or bored; suddenly leave a relationship, your family, your job, etc

head: head towards/for (a place)
head a ball
head a committee/etc
head a list
point: make a point of doing sth
way: get sth out of the way

idioms.meaning: in a straight line (used for measuring distance)
go straight forward, without turning; do what you feel is right in a particular situation, although you are not certain
a long way from any town or city
become confused about where you are and where other things are
go towards someone or something in the quickest and most direct way
away from populated areas or areas popular with tourists
suddenly stop, for example because you are surprised
follow a path that is quicker and shorter than the usual way
follow a longer path than the usual one because it is more attractive

B.title: Complete using the words in bold in the correct form
B.instructions: Complete each paragraph with the correct form of the words in bold.

questions.sentence: The Arctic tern ...... over seventy thousand kilometres each year in the longest migration of any living creature. Unlike seeds that simply ...... on ocean currents, this bird navigates by the stars and the angle of the sun. Scientists have yet to fully explain how each individual recalls its precise ...... across two polar regions year after year.

In tennis, the characteristics of a well-served ball depend heavily on the court surface. On grass, the ball tends to ...... low and fast, making it hard for the receiver to react in time. A skilled player with topspin can make the ball ...... as it travels through the air, dipping sharply before impact. As the ball ...... on its axis at pace, it grips the surface differently from a flat strike and can be virtually unreadable.

C.title: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box
C.instructions: Complete the text with the correct form of the words provided.
C.passage:
ABOARD THE GOLDEN EXPRESS

The golden age of rail travel never truly ended — it simply reinvented itself. While millions still (1) ...... to work on crowded suburban trains, a growing number of adventurous travellers are rediscovering the pleasure of long-distance rail.

Until recently, travelling in genuine comfort by rail meant booking an entire (2) ...... of a private train — an option available only to the very wealthy. A new breed of boutique (3) ...... , specialising in scenic rail experiences, has brought this dream within reach of the dedicated traveller. Rather than offering (4) ...... flights and standard coach packages, these companies provide fully inclusive rail journeys that blend luxury with adventure.

Stops at some of the most spectacular (5) ...... in Europe and beyond are included in every itinerary, and on board, passengers enjoy generous space — very different from the cramped (6) ...... of budget aircraft. Attentive (7) ...... ensure that the food is of restaurant quality and that your every need is attended to as the landscape rolls past outside. And because you sleep while you travel, there is no risk of suffering the exhaustion of (8) ...... to cloud your first morning in a new city.

D.title: Circle the correct word or phrase

K.title: Word formation (Cable Cars for the Modern City)
K.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of the line to form a word that fits in the space.
K.passage:
Cable Cars for the Modern City

It might seem unexpected that a transport system once found only at ski resorts can now serve the …(1)… (UP) levels of urban districts, connecting communities separated by rivers or industrial zones. For commuters with limited …(2)… (MOBILE), crossing a busy road corridor can be practically …(3)… (ACCESS) at peak hours when street-level routes are congested. These are real barriers to daily life — difficulties that must be …(4)… (COME) if cities are to serve all their residents equitably.

Urban cable car technology has …(5)… (GO) considerable development since its first applications in mountainous cities such as La Paz and Medellín. The gondola cars have grown …(6)… (PROGRESS) lighter and more energy-efficient as engineers have adopted new composite materials. Modern control systems can …(7)… (RAPID) detect and respond to changes in wind speed or passenger load, adjusting cable tension in real time. Ensuring the safety of every passenger remains the …(8)… (UP) concern at every stage of design and construction.

The continuous …(9)… (MOVE) of the overhead cable is monitored electronically at each pylon for signs of metal fatigue or stress. The support towers themselves are engineered to …(10)… (STAND) both the mechanical demands of daily operation and the extreme forces generated by high winds or seismic activity. In cities where land is scarce and roads are chronically congested, the cable car has become a genuinely viable and sustainable solution for everyday commuters.
